rows.notes: Page title clearly centered and legible. No amounts visible on this line.
Description legible as Latin form of city name. Numeric columns aligned clearly: '5' in pounds, '13' in shillings, '4' in pence; all values within valid ranges.
Handwritten description likely 'Curia Hastings' (minor letter ambiguity). Pounds column is blank; '1' aligns in shillings column and '8' in pence column, both clear.
Description read as 'Adis Christi Bursario' (some uncertainty in spelling). Amounts '18' and '4' align under shillings and pence; values valid.
College/annuity phrase (some abbreviation in text). Numerals clearly read as 4 | 12 | 6 across pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Description 'Coll Balliol' legible. Pounds column blank; '12' and '6' sit in shillings and pence columns respectively and are unambiguous.
Abbreviated college entry; lettering slightly compressed but readable. Numeric alignment shows shillings 8 and pence 2 with no pounds figure.
Long Latin label for building supervision. A clear '10' appears in the pounds column; no shillings or pence figures visible on this line.
Short description 'Janitori' clear. Amount shows '1' in pounds, '14' shillings and '8' pence aligned cleanly; all within valid ranges.
Library/clerical festival entry; handwriting of description moderately compact but readable. Amounts '10' and '10' align in shillings and pence columns.

rows.description: Eidem ad Fest D Jo Bapt 1844
rows.notes: Similar phrasing to previous line (Eidem ... Bapt.). Amounts '10' and '10' in shillings/pence columns clear; description letters slightly abbreviated.
Text is somewhat cramped and one word uncertain (interpreted as Concionatori ad Fest. Consecrat.); numeric '2' and '2' are visible in shillings/pence columns but handwriting is light.
Repeating 'Eidem ad Fest.' line; date '1844' visible. Amount '2' and '2' in shillings/pence columns clear. Some uncertainty in the intervening abbreviated word.

rows.description: Vicario de Merton ad Fest Annun. 1844
rows.notes: Vicar of Merton entry. Large '40' clearly in pounds column; no shillings or pence shown.
Another 'Eidem' line referencing St Michael; '40' is in pounds column and clear. Slight ambiguity in some letters of description.

rows.description: Vicario de S Newington ad Fest Ann. 1844
rows.notes: Vicar of Newington entry; '40' in pounds column legible. Description contains abbreviations but readable enough to be confident in identification of place and event.
Repeat of a St Michael festival entry. '40' clearly in pounds column. Text is abbreviated but consistent with prior similar lines.

rows.description: Assessed Taxes for the Lodgings April 1844
rows.notes: Long English description fairly legible. Numeric pair '6' (shillings) and '11' (pence) align in shillings/pence columns; pence 11 is valid.
Duplicate/shortened 'Do do' line (October 1844). Amounts align exactly under shillings and pence as previous line: '6' and '11' clear.

rows.description: Land Tax for the City premises
rows.notes: English description clear. '8' appears in pounds column and '15' in shillings column; there is no pence figure. All values valid (shillings < 20).

rows.description: River Water from the City Works
rows.notes: Description legible. '5' in pounds and '5' in shillings columns are clearly written; no pence shown.

rows.description: Insurance of the Coll to Xmas 1844
rows.notes: Clear insurance entry. Numerals '48 | 7 | 6' aligned across pounds/shillings/pence columns and easily readable; all values valid.
Shortened 'Do' prefix then 'Kidlington School'. '1' in pounds and '10' in shillings columns are legible; no pence figure.

rows.description: Gas lights for yr to 2d Day 1844
rows.notes: Gas lights entry readable. Amounts '40 | 13 | 9' visible across the three columns; handwriting of description a bit compressed but clear enough.
Follow-on gas entry 'Do to Michs 1844'. Numerals read as '15' pounds, '18' shillings, '9' pence; all numbers align and are legible.

Place name uncertain (read as 'Yarnton' / 'Garnton' / 'Garinton'); numeric alignment shows '7' in pounds and '6' in shillings. There is a faint small mark in pence column read as '1' but that final digit is less distinct, hence lower confidence.
Description 'Little Ten' legible. Amounts '11 | 8 | 4' are clear across pounds, shillings and pence columns.
Section heading introducing a list of subscriptions. No amounts on this line.
Subscription entry for the infirmary. The first '6' sits under pounds column and the second '6' under shillings — both digits are readable though compact; no pence recorded.
Description legible. A single '20' appears in the pounds column (interpreted as £20); no shillings/pence shown. Alignment clear.
Subscription to Kidlington (abbreviated). '5' in pounds and '5' in shillings columns readable; pence column empty.
Subscription for St Newington (abbreviated). Numerals align as '5' pounds and '5' shillings; description letters slightly abbreviated but identifiable.
Entry for Yarnton (or similar place name shortened with 'Do'). Numbers '3' and '3' clearly in pounds and shillings columns respectively.
Subscription for Merton (abbreviated). '3' and '3' in pounds/shillings are legible and aligned.
Subscription for Garsington (abbreviated). Numerals '3' and '3' readable in pounds and shillings columns; handwriting consistent with prior entries.
Final boxed total at bottom right clearly reads '410 | 8 | 6'. Strong alignment with the three vertical amount columns; digits clear.
